resources.string:
Preprocess the incoming number before it\'s checked by the rules, making it possible to modify rules on the fly.

Such a workflow will be automatically triggered by an incoming call.

Use case:
  Remove extra leading characters from the phone number before screening, as some carriers support dual numbers on a single SIM card.

A typical setup:
- Call Event
- Modify Number
The incoming number %s is modified to %s.

Modify the incoming call number before it\'s checked by the rules.

<b>A use case</b>:
  Some mobile network operators support dual numbers for the same SIM card, they add extra leading digits to the secondary number.
  For example: 3312345679 may become 1233312345679.
  The leading 123 needs to be removed before it\'s checked.

<b>A typical setup:</b>
- Call Event
- Replace Number
  from: ^123
  to: leave as empty

These settings apply to Calls and SMS/MMS actions triggered by Basic Rules, not to Notification Screening.
A Number/Text Rule with its own Notification setting will override this default.
For allowed screened notifications, use Alerts instead.

The built-in notification channels:

<b>None</b>  
  No notification at all.

<b>Low</b>  
  Appears in the status bar and notification shade.

<b>Medium</b>  
  Appears in the status bar and notification shade with a sound.

<b>High</b>  
  Appears in the status bar, notification shade, and as heads-up pop-up with a sound.

<b>High Muted</b>  
  It\'s similar to High but mutes the sound.
  It\'s ideal for Active Chat where continuous sounds might be annoying.
It\'s considered an Active Chat when the screen is unlocked and the SMS app is open in the foreground.
Used instead of the normal SMS channels while you have the SMS app open and the screen unlocked, so incoming messages do not interrupt with a loud sound or heads-up popup.

These settings apply to allowed Notification Screening detections triggered by Basic Rules, not to Calls or SMS/MMS.
A Number/Text Rule with its own Alert setting will override this default.

Choose a custom ringtone, vibration, flashlight, and screen-wake alert that SpamBlocker itself will play when an allowed notification arrives from one of the apps being screened.

This is useful if you have muted the app\'s own notification channel to avoid noise from blocked notifications, since that would otherwise silence allowed ones too.

Each app has its own alert settings, tap the icons to toggle them on/off:
    Vibration
    Flashlight
    Wake Screen

Tap the button on the right to choose a ringtone.
No apps are currently selected for Notification Screening.

Choose a custom ringtone, vibration, flashlight, and screen-wake alert for when THIS rule allows a screened notification.

This is different from the regular Notification setting above: since the original notification is already visible on screen, allowing it again does not need a duplicate SpamBlocker notification, just a short alert to draw attention.

Leave this as Default to use the app-level alert configured under Basic Rules Notification.

This ID will appear in system settings as the notification channel.

Once created, the ID cannot be edited.
The importance can only be set when creating a new channel, once created, it can only be modified in system settings.

When left empty, notifications are automatically grouped into three categories:

- Spam call
- Spam SMS
- Valid SMS

If a group is specified, they will be grouped separately.

The sound can only be set when creating a new channel, once created, it can only be modified in system settings.

Custom Sound
Some phones allow direct import of custom ringtones via the app. For others, import through system settings and then select it in the app.

Create a new notification channel.

Long press a notification channel in this dropdown list to edit it.

This channel is currently used by the following rules.

%s

Make sure to update them after deleting this channel.
The notification channel will be silenced by force, even if it\'s configured as high importance.
The light color can only be set when creating a new channel, once created, it can only be modified in system settings.
Tap the ARGB text to manually edit it.
